item.title: 创建响应式网站设计。 | Creating Responsive Website Design.
item.description:
制作响应式网页有时可以...

响应式网页设计的目标是让网页在所有设备上看起来都很好。

响应式网页设计将自动调整或适应不同的屏幕尺寸和视口。

在我创建响应式网页的小研究中，
我找到了一些宝石来帮助我们创建响应式设计😂😐

使用 HTML 的响应式网站设计方法

您可以通过向所有 html 网页添加<meta>标记来实现响应能力。

这将设置页面的视口。 换句话说，它将为浏览器提供有关如何控制页面尺寸和缩放的说明。

如果 CSS 宽度属性设置为 100%，图像将响应并放大和缩小。
也就是说，如果图像最大宽度为 100%，则图像将在必要时缩小，但永远不会放大到大于原始尺寸。

可以使用“视口宽度”或“vw”单位设置文本大小。

这样，文本大小将跟随浏览器窗口的大小。

使用 CSS 的响应式网站设计方法

在我们的主容器中建立一个简单的<display:flex;>布局，并通过移动和桌面的媒体查询进一步设置样式，可以轻松创建一个响应式网页。

阅读更多关于Flex 布局的信息

使用媒体查询，您可以为不同的浏览器尺寸定义完全不同的样式。

查找有关媒体查询的更多信息

我知道🥲


回头见。



原文:

Making a Responsive webpage can sometime go...

The goal of a Responsive web design is about making web pages look good on all devices.

A responsive web design will automatically adjust or adapt to different screen sizes and viewports.

In my little research to creating a responsive webpage,
I found some gems to help us create responsive designs😂😐

Responsive Website Design method using HTML

↓↓
1. Using the <meta> tag
You can achieve responsiveness by adding <meta>tag to all your html web pages.

The <meta>tag forces the web page to follow the screen-width of the device.

This will set the viewport of your page. In order words, it'll give the browser instructions on how to control the page's dimensions and scaling.

↓↓
2. Using the <picture> tag

If the CSS width property is set to 100%, the image will be responsive and scale up and down.
That is, If the image max-width is at 100%, the image will scale down if it has to, but never scale up to be larger than the original size.

The HTML element allows you to define different images for different browser window sizes.

↓↓
3: Responsive Text Size
The text size can be set with a "viewport width" or"vw" unit.

In that way, the text size would follow the size of the browser window.

Responsive Website Design method using CSS

↓↓
1: Flex Layout
Establishing a simple <display:flex;> layout in our main container and further styling with media query for mobile and desktop can easily create a responsive webpage.

Read more on Flex Layout

↓↓
2: Media query

With media queries you can define completely different styles for different browser sizes.

Find more on media query

I know🥲


See you later.
item.author: prudence97

item.title: 烤代码#1 | 剪刀石头布 | Roast the code #1 | Rock Paper Scissors
item.description:
嘿！ 我正在开始一个名为“Roast the Code”的新系列，我将在其中分享一些代码，让您烘焙和改进它。

没有更多的，只是要有礼貌和建设性，这是一个练习，所以我们可以一起学习。

现在，前往回购并尽可能努力地烤！

嘿！ 这个存储库是 Rock Paper Scissors 游戏的实现。

我对这段代码的目标是在 DEV.to 上发布一篇文章，让人们烤它，如果他们愿意，可以改进它！

入门

  1. 克隆回购
  2. 将目录更改为克隆的 repo
  3. 安装 deps npm install
  4. 运行游戏npm start
  5. 代码从main.ts开始
  6. 烤代码！

怎么烤？

前往 DEV.to，并在帖子上发表评论。 如果您愿意，您也可以在此处发送带有您提议的更改的 PR！




如果您希望您的代码在该系列中被烘烤，请在评论中与我联系或在 DEV 上发送我的 PM。 我将在该系列的未来部分中分享它！



原文:

Hey there! 👋

I'm starting a new series called "Roast the Code", where I will share some code, and let YOU roast and improve it.

There's not much more to it, just be polite and constructive, this is an exercise so we can all learn together.

Now then, head over to the repo and roast as hard as you can!!

Hey there! 👋 This repository it's an implementation of the Rock Paper Scissors game.

My goal with this code is to publish a post over on DEV.to and let people roast it, and improve it if they feel like it!

Getting Started

  1. Clone repo
  2. Change diretory to the cloned repo
  3. Install deps npm install
  4. Run the game npm start
  5. Code starts at main.ts
  6. Roast the code!

How to roast?

Head over to DEV.to, and leave a coment on the post. If you want to, you can also send in a PR with your proposed changes here!




If you want your code to be roasted on the series, please contact me in the comments or send my a PM here on DEV. And I will share it in a future instalment of the series!
item.author: nombrekeff

item.title: 为什么我们讨厌…… | Why We Hate…
item.description:
有时我会花一些时间尝试获得新的编程技能，最终发现我是在浪费时间做一些无用的事情。

但即使是著名的编程工具和语言也有自己的问题，这就是我们讨厌它们的原因。

为什么我们讨厌 Python

当 Python 允许更改变量类型时，它也可能允许将错误从引起错误的地方带走，这是调试中最糟糕的情况。

为什么我们讨厌 Ruby

您在编程中可以想象的所有错误，您都可以在 Ruby 中找到。

它还促进猴子补丁、代码注入、资产隐式导出和工具垄断。 几乎不可能追踪不良行为的来源。

你可能会称 Ruby 为调试地狱。

但是，它非常适合 PoC。

为什么我们讨厌 Java

与 Python 和 Ruby 不同，Java 是静态类型的，如果您不希望它们在出现的地方爆炸，则需要明确地携带错误。

尽管如此，Java 仍然非常官僚，最简单的程序很难完成，并且需要许多代码语句。

此外，Java 非常贪婪，会消耗它所能占用的所有硬件资源。

为什么我们讨厌 Scala

您期望从不纯的函数式编程语言中获得的每一个好的和有用的特性，都来自 Scala。

然而，Scala 编译为 JVM 字节码 😖 – 这就是不使用它的充分理由。

有人可以说你可以做Scala Native ，但那是你不想进入的地狱——相信我。

为什么我们讨厌 OCaml

更简单的项目比 Java 更官僚，你很快就会陷入循环引用的地狱。

为什么我们讨厌标准机器学习

但标准机器学习还有另一个非常烦人的问题：缺乏工具。

你可以在 OCaml 中找到几乎所有你需要的库，而你找不到的，你可以从 C 中绑定。标准 ML 在库上要差得多，而且你很容易错过一些东西。

为什么我们讨厌 C/C++

您需要的一切，都可以在 C/C++ 中找到或构建。

但是 C 太冗长了，一切都很难完成，C++ 是一个无限标记的陷阱，并且两者都有意想不到的行为。 不要误会我的意思！ 对这些语言的更好描述可能是：C/C++ 完全按照您的要求做，而不是您想要的。

为什么我们讨厌朱莉娅

关于Julia ，我认为我们不需要走得更远：

当您将值分配给常量时，会引发警告而不是错误的编程语言会发生什么？ 而且程序还在继续！

为什么我们讨厌尼姆

或者它会是……

大尼姆的禁忌是，你不断地碰到不按他们说的那样行事的事情——或者根本不工作。

为什么我们讨厌 Elixir

在过去的某个时候，Erlang 变得比 Ruby 更酷，所以所有酷孩子都想用它编写代码，但他们做不到。 糟糕的程序员无法处理 BEAM 的限制。 为了让 then 与 Erlang 一起工作，一些低能开发人员决定绕过 BEAM 限制，消除所有 Erlang 优势——但它仍然很酷。 这就是 Elixir：一种消除 BEAM 优势的解决方法。

为什么我们讨厌 X

我可以继续讨论Javascript 、 TypeScript 、 Wren 、 Odin 、 Moonscript 、 Lua 、 Dart 、 C# 、 F★和所有其他值得注意的编程语言，但我认为我们在这里很好。



原文:

Original post in Kodumaro.

Sometimes I spend some time trying to acquire new programming skills, to finally figure out I was wasting time with something useless.

But even well-known programming tools and languages have their own issues, and this is why we hate them.

Why we hate Python

Python is one of my favourite programming languages, but I haven’t work with it for a while, and it isn’t for nothing.

Python is strongly typed, but also dynamically typed – which wouldn’t be a problem if it respected the assigned types, but you can assign an integer to a variable and then assign a named tuple right after that.

Erlang has a very useful line: “fail fast and noisily.” When Python allows to change variable types, it potentially allows to carry errors away from where they were caused too, which is the worse situation in a debug.

Why we hate Ruby

Ruby is one of the most beloved toy programming languages, mainly because it makes bad programmers to look cool. Everything wrong you can imagine in programming, you can find in Ruby.

Ruby falls in the same Python’s typing issues. It also promotes monkey-patch, code injection, assets’ implicit exportation, and tool monopoly. It’s nearly impossible to track down where a bad behaviour comes from.

You might call Ruby a debugging hell.

However, it’s great for PoC.

Why we hate Java

Java is the big static companies’ darling.

Unlike Python and Ruby, Java is statically typed, and you need to carry errors explicitly if you don’t want them to explode right where they arise.

Still, Java is extremely bureaucratic though, the simplest procedure is hard to be done and requires many code statements.

Besides, Java is very greedy, consuming every hardware resource it can take.

Why we hate Scala

The worst flaw of Scala is not to be Dotty. Every nice and useful feature you expect from an impure functional programming language, you get from Scala.

However, Scala compiles to JVM bytecodes 😖 – and this is enough reason not to use it.

One can say you could do Scala Native, but that’s a hell you don’t wanna get in – believe me.

Why we hate OCaml

OCaml is the perfect impure functional programming language, except for requiring widely long boilerplates to work.

The simpler project is more bureaucratic than Java, and you can get into a hell of cyclical references too soon.

Why we hate Standard ML

An alternative to OCaml is its grandpa, Standard ML, which doesn’t have the OCaml’s same issues.

But Standard ML has another one, very annoying: lack of tools.

You can find libraries for almost everything you need in OCaml, and what you can’t find, you can bind from C. Standard ML is way poorer on libraries, and you can get in trouble missing something very easily.

Why we hate C/C++

C and C++ are great programming languages, so great that lots of other programming languages are built on top of them. Everything you need, you can find or build in C/C++.

But C is much too verbose, everything is tough to get done, C++ is a pitfall of infinite tokens, and both have unexpected behaviour. Don’t get me wrong! C/C++ are very stable, but they simply don’t behave like you may expect.

The better description for those languages could be: C/C++ do exactly what you ask, not what you want.

Why we hate Julia

Regarding Julia, I think we don’t need to go further than this:

What to expect from a programming language that, when you assign a value to a constant, raises a warning instead of an error? And the program keeps going!

Why we hate Nim

Nim is Python made right. Or it would be…

The big Nim’s no-no is that you keep bumping into things that don’t behave as they told – or don’t work at all.

The Nim’s line is: WORKS ON MY MACHINE.

Why we hate Elixir

Elixir is a toy programming language created because kids can’t code in Erlang – but Erlang is cool, and they wanna look cool.

BEAM is the Erlang’s virtual machine, and it has a lotta restrictions that ensure your app isn’t gonna crash unexpectedly.

At some point in the past, Erlang had become cooler than Ruby, so all cool kids wanted to code in it, but they aren’t able to. Bad programmers can’t deal with the BEAM’s restrictions. 🤷

In order to allow then to work with Erlang, some imbecile developer decided to work around the BEAM restrictions, obliterating all Erlang advantages – but it’s still cool. This is Elixir: a workaround to obliterate BEAM advantages.

Why we hate X

I could keep going on and on, talking about Javascript, TypeScript, Wren, Odin, Moonscript, Lua, Dart, C#, F★, and every other noteworthy programming language, but I think we’re good here.
item.author: cacilhas

item.title: 使用 Lambda、DynamoDB、API Gateway、Cognito、S3 和 CloudFront 在无服务器上创建实例调度程序 | Create Instance Scheduler on Serverless by using Lambda, DynamoDB, API Gateway, Cognito,S3 and CloudFront
item.description:
通过使用实例调度程序，您可以启动/停止或调度 EC2/RDS 实例以节省成本。

实例调度程序是开源的，基于 AWS 无服务器架构。

为什么我使用无服务器？
因为：

  • 没有要管理的服务器。
  • 永远不要为闲置资源付费，只为每个事件付费。
  • 以弹性和灵活性进行扩展。
  • 快速发展。

应用架构

在应用架构中：
前端
用户从CloudFront服务请求内容以获取 HTML、CSS 和 javascript。 后端
一旦前端在用户的互联网浏览器上本地加载，然后用户注册或登录到Cognito服务，它会向用户响应一个 JWT 令牌。

用户可以使用 JWT 令牌对API 网关执行 HTTP 方法 GET/POST/PUT/DELETE，API 网关从 Cognito 服务检查 JWT 令牌是否有效，然后使用请求调用lambda函数。

此外，lambda 与密钥管理服务(KMS) 交互以加密/解密来自/进入 dynamoDB 的敏感数据。
在电子邮件通知的情况下，lambda 使用简单电子邮件服务(SES) 向用户发送电子邮件。

数据库数据保存在 dynamoDB 中。

用户的电子邮件是分区键，并且创建了排序键，电子邮件始终是唯一的，并且可以使用[日期或帐户信息或aws键]更改排序键。


还使用名为dtime的属性作为全局二级索引 GSI 来获取特定日期和时间的所有用户。

调度器
部署应用程序的步骤：
您需要拥有aws cli并配置aws access key 和 secret keys 。

在本地克隆应用程序

git clone https://github.com/maradwan/instance-scheduler.git

获取 instance-scheduler 的 UserPools 的 id 并创建用户池客户端

aws cognito-idp create-user-pool-client --client-name my-user-pool-client --user-pool-id eu-west-1_2z7VlI6nq

记下UserPoolId , ClientId

 { "UserPoolClient": { "UserPoolId": "eu-west-1_2z7VlI6nq", "ClientName": "my-user-pool-client", "ClientId": "gqmnvsr3e8jmbg6ptaul30v9g", "LastModifiedDate": 1653128931.722, "CreationDate": 1653128931.722, "RefreshTokenValidity": 30, "AllowedOAuthFlowsUserPoolClient": false } }

记下 Cognito 服务的arn

 aws cognito-idp describe-user-pool --user-pool-id eu-west-1_2z7VlI6nq

创建自定义域名，必须是唯一的名称

aws cognito-idp create-user-pool-domain --domain instance-scheduler --user-pool-id eu-west-1_2z7VlI6nq

您将收到一封来自“亚马逊网络服务”的电子邮件，您需要单击该链接来验证您的电子邮件。

检查您的电子邮件是否已通过验证

{ "Identities": [ "your_email_address@example.com" ] }

编辑此文件scheduler-lambda/scheduler-lambda.tf并更改以下内容：

  • 编辑此文件app/zappa_settings.json并更改以下内容：

    • 在步骤 2 中将“provider_arns”添加到您的 Cognito ARN
    • 在步骤 2 中将“USERPOOLID”和“COGNITOR_CLIENTID”添加到您的 Cognito UserPoolId 和 ClientId
    • 命名的“s3_bucket”存储桶应该是全局唯一的
    • 在第 4 步中将“KMS_KEY_ID”添加到您的 KMS id

    之后应用以下命令：

     virtualenv -p python3 env3 source env3/bin/activate pip install -r app/requirements.txt cd app zappa deploy

    记下您的API 网关看起来像这样

    Your updated Zappa deployment is live!: https://74628jl936.execute-api.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/staging

    将以下角色添加到scheduler-lambda/kms.tf ，允许使用密钥和允许附加持久资源的“Sid”

     ["arn:aws:iam::${local.account_id}:role/instance-scheduler-staging-ZappaLambdaExecutionRole", "${aws_iam_role.lambda_role.arn}"]

    之后再次应用 terraform

     cd scheduler-lambda terraform apply

    该脚本创建 S3 和 CloudFront，然后将 html 文件夹同步到存储桶。

    编辑此文件html/js/config.js并更改以下内容：

    • 步骤 2 中的 userPoolId、“userPoolClientId”和“region”到您的 UserPoolId、ClientId 以及您使用的区域
    • 在步骤 5 中调用您的 API 网关它看起来像这样：
     window._config = { cognito: { userPoolId: 'eu-west-1_XXX', userPoolClientId: 'XXX', region: 'eu-west-1' }, api: { invokeUrl: 'https://api.example.com' } };

    然后运行脚本

    等待 5 分钟，直到部署 CloudFront，然后登录到 URL。

    测试 APP创建一个新的 IAM 用户，仅用于编程访问并分配以下策略。 替换 REGION_NAME、ACCOUNT_NUMBER 和 INSTANCE_ID

     { "Version": "2012-10-17", "Statement": [ { "Effect": "Allow", "Action": [ "ec2:StartInstances", "ec2:StopInstances" ], "Resource": "arn:aws:ec2:REGION_NAME:ACCOUNT_NUMBER:instance/INSTANCE_ID" } ] }

    对于 RDS 策略

    { "Version": "2012-10-17", "Statement": [ { "Sid": "VisualEditor0", "Effect": "Allow", "Action": [ "RDS:StopDBInstance", "RDS:StartDBInstance" ], "Resource": [ "arn:aws:rds:REGION_NAME:ACCOUNT_NUMBER:db:DB_NAME" ] } ] }

    • 在应用程序中创建密钥

    • 添加 EC2 和 RDS 实例

    W. Lee Pang, PhD - W. Lee Pang 博士 - Apr 26 4月26日



原文:

By using an instance scheduler you can start/stop or scheduler EC2/RDS instances to save costs.

The instance scheduler is open source and based on AWS serverless architecture.

Why do I use serverless?
Because:

  • No servers to manage.
  • Never pay for idler resources, only pay per event.
  • Scale with resilience and flexibility.
  • Fast development.

The App Architecture

In the application architecture:
Frontend
The user requests content from CloudFront service to get the HTML, CSS, and javascript. CloudFront caches the static files from the S3.
Backend
Once the Frontend is loaded locally on the user's internet browser, then the user SignUp or SignIn to the Cognito service, and it responded with a JWT token to the user.

The user can perform HTTP methods GET/POST/PUT/DELETE to the API gateway with the JWT token, API Gateway checks if the JWT token is valid from the Cognito service and after that invoke the lambda function with the request.

The lambda checks the events and extracts the user's email from the JWT token and add/update/delete the user's data from/to dynamoDB.
Also, lambda interacts with Key Management Service(KMS) to encrypt/decrypt sensitive data from/into dynamoDB.
In the case of email notification, lambda uses Simple Email Service (SES) to send emails to the users.

Database The data is saved in dynamoDB.

The user's email is the partition key, and the sort key is created, always email is unique and the sort key can be changed with [ date or account information or aws keys].


Also used the attribute named dtime as Global Secondary Indexes GSI to get all users by specific date and time.

Scheduler
CloudWatch is used as a cron job to run each hour, it invokes the lambda function to get all attributes from Global Secondary Indexes GSI (dtime) of dynamoDB with specific dates and time, and it performs which EC2/RDS instances need to start/stop, after that will notify the user by email or webhook.

Steps to deploy the App:
You need to have aws cli and configured aws access key and secret keys.

Clone the app locally by

git clone https://github.com/maradwan/instance-scheduler.git

1- Create a dynamoDB table

aws dynamodb create-table \
    --table-name instance-scheduler \
    --attribute-definitions AttributeName=email,AttributeType=S AttributeName=created,AttributeType=S AttributeName=dtime,AttributeType=S\
    --key-schema AttributeName=email,KeyType=HASH AttributeName=created,KeyType=RANGE \
    --provisioned-throughput ReadCapacityUnits=1,WriteCapacityUnits=1 \
    --global-secondary-indexes "IndexName=dtime-index,\
    KeySchema=[{AttributeName=dtime,KeyType=HASH}],\
    Projection={ProjectionType=ALL},\
    ProvisionedThroughput={ReadCapacityUnits=1,WriteCapacityUnits=1}"

2- Create Cognito service

aws cognito-idp create-user-pool --pool-name instance-scheduler --schema Name=email,Required=true --auto-verified-attributes email --verification-message-template DefaultEmailOption=CONFIRM_WITH_LINK

aws cognito-idp list-user-pools --max-results 20

Get the id of UserPools of instance-scheduler and create the user pool client

aws cognito-idp create-user-pool-client --client-name my-user-pool-client --user-pool-id eu-west-1_2z7VlI6nq

Take a note of the UserPoolId, ClientId

{
    "UserPoolClient": {
        "UserPoolId": "eu-west-1_2z7VlI6nq",
        "ClientName": "my-user-pool-client",
        "ClientId": "gqmnvsr3e8jmbg6ptaul30v9g",
        "LastModifiedDate": 1653128931.722,
        "CreationDate": 1653128931.722,
        "RefreshTokenValidity": 30,
        "AllowedOAuthFlowsUserPoolClient": false
    }
}

Take a note arn of the Cognito service

aws cognito-idp describe-user-pool --user-pool-id eu-west-1_2z7VlI6nq

Create custom domain name, should be unique name

aws cognito-idp create-user-pool-domain --domain instance-scheduler --user-pool-id eu-west-1_2z7VlI6nq

3- Verify your email from Simple Email Service (SES) to used for sending notifications

aws ses verify-email-identity --email-address your_email_address@example.com --region eu-west-1

You will receive an email from "Amazon Web Services" you need to click on the link to verify your email.

Check your email if has been verified by

{
    "Identities": [
        "your_email_address@example.com"
    ]
}

4- Create hourly Scheduler Lambda, KMS and Cloudwatch event by using terraform.
edit this file scheduler-lambda/scheduler-lambda.tf and change the following:

  • "SOURCE_EMAIL" to your verified email in step 4
  • "REGION_NAME" to which region you are using after that apply the following commands and take a note of kms key_id: 
cd scheduler-lambda
terraform init
terraform apply

5- Create API Gateway and Lambda by using Zappa

edit this file app/zappa_settings.json and change the following:

  • "provider_arns" to your Cognito ARN in the step 2
  • "USERPOOLID" and "COGNITOR_CLIENTID" to your Cognito UserPoolId and ClientId in step 2
  • "SOURCE_EMAIL" to your verified email in step 3
  • "s3_bucket" bucket named should be unique globally
  • "REGION_NAME" to which region you are using
  • "KMS_KEY_ID" to your KMS id in the step 4

after that apply the following commands:

virtualenv -p python3 env3
source env3/bin/activate
pip install -r app/requirements.txt
cd app
zappa deploy

Take a note of your API Gateway looks like this

Your updated Zappa deployment is live!: https://74628jl936.execute-api.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/staging

6- Update the KMS to allow the lambda in step 5 to encrypt/decrypt

Add the following role to scheduler-lambda/kms.tf , "Sid" of Allow use of the key and Allow attachment of persistent resources

["arn:aws:iam::${local.account_id}:role/instance-scheduler-staging-ZappaLambdaExecutionRole", "${aws_iam_role.lambda_role.arn}"]

After that apply terraform again

cd scheduler-lambda
terraform apply

7- Create CloudFront and S3 The bucket should be a unique name, edit the cloudfront-s3-sync.sh and change the bucket name and region name. The script creates S3 and CloudFront then sync html folder to the bucket.

Edit this file html/js/config.js and change the following:

  • userPoolId, "userPoolClientId" and "region" to your UserPoolId, ClientId in step 2 and which region you are using
  • invokeUrl to your API Gateway in step 5 It looks like this: 
window._config = {
    cognito: {
        userPoolId: 'eu-west-1_XXX',
        userPoolClientId: 'XXX',
        region: 'eu-west-1'
    },
    api: {
         invokeUrl: 'https://api.example.com'

    }
};

Then run the script

Wait 5 minutes until CloudFront be deployed and then login to the URL.

Test the APP Create a new IAM user only for programmatic access and assign the the following policy. replace REGION_NAME, ACCOUNT_NUMBER and INSTANCE_ID

{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
        {
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "ec2:StartInstances",
                "ec2:StopInstances"
            ],
            "Resource": "arn:aws:ec2:REGION_NAME:ACCOUNT_NUMBER:instance/INSTANCE_ID"

        }
    ]
}

For RDS policy

{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
        {
            "Sid": "VisualEditor0",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "RDS:StopDBInstance",
                "RDS:StartDBInstance"
            ],
            "Resource": [
                "arn:aws:rds:REGION_NAME:ACCOUNT_NUMBER:db:DB_NAME"
            ]
        }
    ]
}

  • Create a key in the app

  • Add the EC2 and RDS instances

W. Lee Pang, PhD - Apr 26
item.author: maradwan

item.title: 学习 Golang - 第 1 天 | Learning Golang - day 1
item.description:
我刚决定重新开始学习围棋。 去年秋天我学了一点，但后来我的个人生活过得很艰难，还决定开始寻找一份使用 vanilla JavaScript 的前端工作。 我找到了一份工作，我可以在工作中学到任何我需要的东西。 所以在我的空闲时间，下班后，我想专注于一些不同的事情。

我的计划是每天学习一点，最好做一些笔记。 我有很多资料——书籍和在线课程。 我还在文档中阅读了很多关于 Go 文档和教程的内容。 为了不与分析瘫痪作斗争，我想从 Head First Go 开始一次使用一个资源。

我今天学到的东西：

这意味着当我们创建一个变量时，我们应该声明它的类型（string、boolean、int、float64 等），虽然我们可以更改变量的值，但我们不能稍后在程序中更改它的类型。

这样，每个变量的值为 0（对于字符串 0 表示空字符串，对于布尔值则为 false）

现在，它知道 city 是一个字符串，length 是一个 float64

如果我们想要进行数学计算，我们不能在 Go 中将整数添加到浮点数。 我们需要将整数转换为浮点数。 我们也可以将浮点数转换为整数，但它可以更改其值。

 allApples := 3 eatenApples := 0.5 applesLeft := float64(allApples) - eatenApples fmt.Println(applesLeft)

否则，我们会出错。 相同的导入但未使用的包。

但是，如果我们想让我们的包在另一个程序中使用成为可能，并使一些变量在那里可用，我们需要从大写开始。

到目前为止，我一直在使用“fmt”包来打印我的代码输出，这要归功于 Println 方法。

这主要是我在秋天学到的东西的重复。 但我认为这是一个好的开始。



原文:

I just decided to start learning Go again. I was learning it a bit last autumn, but then I had a very rough time in my personal life and also decided to start looking for a frontend job with vanilla JavaScript. I got a job where I can learn on the job anything I need for it. So in my free time, after work, I want to focus on something a bit different.

My plan is to learn a bit each day and preferably take some notes. I have quite a lot of materials - books, and online courses. I also read a lot good about Go documentation and tutorial in the docs. In order not to struggle with analysis paralysis, I want to use one resource at a time starting from Head First Go.

Things I learned today:

1. Main structure of a file in Go: 

package main
// the name of the package - we call the function the same import ( "fmt" "reflect"
) // Here we import other packages we are going to use in our code. func main() { fmt.Println("Hello, Go!") fmt.Println(reflect.TypeOf("Hello, Go!"))
} // Then goes the function with its body between moustache brackets.

2. Go is a strictly typed language.

It means that when we are creating a variable we should declare its type (string, boolean, int, float64 and so on) and while we can change the value of the variable, we can't change its type later on in the program.

3. There are at least 3 ways of declaring variables:

- we can declare a variable without assigning any value to it

var name string;
var age int;
var price float64;

This way, the value of each variable is 0 (for strings 0 means an empty string, for boolean it's false)

** - when we declare a variable and assign a value, we don't have to declare the type because Go recognizes the type from the value**

var city = "Warsaw"
var length = 3.5

Now, it knows that city is a string and length is a float64

** - while declaring the variable with its value, we can use a shorter declaration form**

quantity := 4
fruit := "apple"

4. We can convert types.

If we want to make mathematical calculations we can't add an integer to a float number in Go. We need to convert an integer into a float number. We can also convert a float number into an integer but it can change its value.

allApples := 3
eatenApples := 0.5
applesLeft := float64(allApples) - eatenApples
fmt.Println(applesLeft)

5. If we declare a variable, we need to use it.

Otherwise, we will have an error. The same imported but unused packages.

6. Variables and functions usually start with a lowercase.

But if we want to make it possible to use our package in another program and make some of the variables available there, we need to start them from an uppercase.

So far I've been using "fmt" package for printing the output of my code and it was possible thanks to the Println method.

It's been mostly the repetition of what I learned in the autumn. But I think it's a good start.
item.author: makneta

item.title: Web 应用颜色助手 | Web App Color Helper
item.description:
任务是使用一个 fetch 调用和一些事件侦听器来制作一个基本的 Web 应用程序。 我决定使用颜色 API 来获取有关在使用应用程序时选择的颜色的一些信息。

这里的想法是使用基本的 HTML 输入颜色类型，并返回有关所选颜色的一些详细信息，例如各种格式（CMYK、HEX、RGB）的值，以及一些可能与您在任何项目中选择的颜色一起使用的互补颜色你正在努力。

在补充颜色旁边还有一个复制按钮，可用于将值复制到剪贴板，也可能复制到您的代码中。

最大限度地构建和测试我的新手编码技能很有趣。 获取过程非常顺利，我很容易地更新了 DOM。 事实证明，最让我头疼的是引导程序和复制按钮上的事件侦听器。

我以前从未使用过引导程序，但很快就开始掌握它的窍门。 乍一看，它使 HTML 看起来有点忙，但我知道它在清理 CSS 方面做了很多工作。 事实上，我不需要专门的 CSS 页面来构建这个简单的小工具； 事后看来，bootstrap 很酷（有时....）

获取复制按钮的事件侦听器非常棘手。 我发现循环可以将事件监听器添加到我在 DOM 中渲染它们时创建的每个按钮。 然后问题来了

我意识到整个异步 JavaScript 真的是一把双刃剑

因此，长话短说，我需要等待我的 fetch 调用完成并呈现页面，然后我的查询选择器可以获取内部 HTML 正在创建的 copyBTN 类。 事后现在写，这似乎是一个明显而简单的解决方案，很好的 ole Async Await 来拯救这个。

我认为从这个小型入门项目中最大的收获是时间管理，并意识到可以在此过程中进行调整。 在我写这篇文章的时候，innerHTML 被用来渲染页面的一部分意味着需要做更多的工作。 虽然这在某种程度上感觉像是未完成的事情，但它也为我在通往 Web 开发伟大的漫长旅程中奠定了下一个小小的胜利。



原文:

The assignment was to use a fetch call and a few event listeners to make a basic web app. I decided to use the color API in order to fetch some info about a color that was selected while using the app.

The idea here is to use a basic HTML input type of color and get back some details about the color selected like its value in various formats (CMYK, HEX, RGB) and also some complimentary colors that might work with your selected color on whatever project you are working on.

There is also a copy button next to the complimentary colors that can be used to copy the value to the clip board and possibly to your code.

This was fun to build and tested my newbie coding skills to the max. The fetch went pretty smooth, I got the DOM updated fairly easily. The big headaches for me proved to be bootstrap and the event listeners on the copy button.

I had never used bootstrap before but began to get the hang of it fairly quickly. At a first glance it makes the HTML seem a little busy but I understand it does so much to clean up your CSS. As a matter of fact I did not need dedicated CSS page to build this simple little tool; in hindsight bootstrap is pretty cool to work with (at times....)

Getting the event listeners for the copy button was super tricky. I discovered that a loop could add the event listener to each button that I was creating when I was rendering them in the DOM. Then the problem hit and

I realized what a double edged sword this whole asynchronous JavaScript really was

So to make a long story short I needed to WAIT for my fetch call to complete and render the page BEFORE my query selector could grab the copyBTN class that the inner HTML was creating. Writing it now after the fact, it seems like such an obvious and simple solution, good ole Async Await to the rescue on this one.

I think the biggest take away from this little starter project is time management and realizing it is okay to tweak things along the way. As I am writing this the fact that innerHTML is being used to render part of the page means that more work is needed. While this feels like unfinished business in a way it also sets up my next little victory on my long journey to Web Development greatness.
item.author: jsignature

item.title: 好笔记的重要性... | The importance of good notes...
item.description:
良好的笔记记录是在许多专业环境中取得成功的关键，但它在 IT 中是必不可少的，因为我也在 Web 开发中学习。 记住所有内容是不可能的，拥有一套好的笔记可以使过程更顺畅，重复性更少。 他们说 Google 是开发人员最好的朋友，老实说，这是我听过的最有效的观察之一。 谷歌搜索很棒，但一次又一次地四处寻找相同的信息很快就会变老......

我知道我在某个地方看到过，等等，它一定是在 w3 学校，不，我猜是 Mozilla，我喜欢这个网站，但我希望它有一个黑暗模式，这个网站很棒，但每次我看的时候布局都超级混乱为了某件事

作为大型数据中心的网络技术人员，我每天都会使用我的笔记，但并非总是如此。 老实说，学习新事物和维护一个合适的笔记本是一件非常痛苦的事情。 让我分享对我有用的东西。 这个信息不是惊天动地的，我写这篇文章是为了提醒你没有笔记，至少是好的笔记。

笔和纸还是数字？

这很简单，你必须数字化。 有很多很棒的应用程序都有自己的优点和缺点。 这些是我使用的一些：

如果我必须推荐一个入门并且您愿意陷入困境，我会尝试 Clickup。 我说很深，因为它不完全是为了记笔记。 这是一个超级强大的组织套件，提供了一种叫做 ClickUp docs 的东西，这对于笔记来说非常棒。 它在设备之间完美同步，有一个桌面应用程序，简单地说它就可以工作。 有几个 YouTube 视频解释了整个平台，非常值得一看。 你可以插入代码块并在markdown中编写，就我这里做的！ 哦，顺便说一句，ClickUp 基本版是免费的，请检查一下，准备好感到惊讶和有点害怕...... https://clickup.com/features/docs

如果您想要更简单、也超级可靠并且只关注笔记的东西，Microsoft OneNote 是一个不错的选择。 开箱即用，使用起来很直观，它们的搜索功能可能是这些选项中最好的。 您可以通过超级强大的全局搜索在笔记本和章节之间快速解析。

我偏爱 Coda 只是因为我使用它的方式更多，而且我喜欢与 Google 轻松集成的 Coda Pack； 概念也有这些东西，就像我说的只是我选择一个......

所以我必须手动输入所有内容？

不，甚至没有接近。 进入屏幕截图工具。 在 Windows 上是 [Start Key (on keyboard) + Shift + S]，在 Mac [Shift + Command + 5] 上，您需要记住这些组合键。 剪切并粘贴到您最喜欢的笔记应用程序中。

暂停视频，剪断。 在 Mozilla 文档中查看大量代码，snip。 输入你需要在 VS Code 中记住的天才，剪断。 剪辑是一切的开始！

现在除了剪辑之外，还有更多需要注意的地方，记住如果你要发布的东西不要忘记获取源信息，以便在应得的地方给予适当的信任！ 您可以制作标题（最终将出现在目录中），突出显示代码，将其放入代码块中，有些程序甚至允许语法突出显示。 这是来自我在 ClickUp 中的 javascript 笔记本。

链接哦链接。 是的，链接是关键，继续并嵌入或粘贴到最喜欢的 YouTube 视频的链接中，该视频解释了 JavaScript 中的值传递与引用传递。 是的，这是你最后一次需要观看它，因为这次你得到了它，但以防万一在你的笔记中放一个链接。

是这样吗？

是的！ 复制粘贴文本、屏幕截图、链接，然后输入其余内容。 你自然会发展出你喜欢的分手方式。 例如，您可以有一个关于 JavaScript 的主页，然后是关于数组操作、DOM 选择器和事件侦听器的子页面。 将这些页面与标题分开，以便您可以快速跳转到该区域，或者您可以使用其中许多应用程序的全局搜索功能。 找到你需要的东西，希望它在一个很好的代码块中，你可以复制并粘贴到 VScode 中！

总之，如果您发现自己不止一次地在谷歌上搜索同一件事或研究新材料，请尝试做一些笔记。

老实说，要做到这一点需要时间，但最终还是值得的。

目前我正在参加一个编码训练营，我有两个优秀的讲师和一个写得很好的课程，这些课程总是链接到外部参考，这些最终会在我凌晨 3 点需要它们时出现在我的笔记中......

下次当您学习新知识并准备为自己感到自豪时，请尝试一下！



原文:

Good note taking is key to succeeding in many professional settings but it is essential in IT and as I am learning in Web Development too. It is impossible to remember everything and having a good set of notes to fall back on makes the process smoother and less repetitive. They say that Google is a developers best friend which is honestly one of the most valid observations I have ever heard. Googling is great but hunting around for the same info time after time gets old quick...

I know I saw this someplace, Wait it must have been on w3 schools, Nope I guess it was Mozilla, I love this site but I wish it had a dark mode, This site is awesome but the layout is super confusing every time I look for something

As a Network Technician in a large data center I utilize my notes daily, but it wasn't always this way. To be honest learning new things and maintaining a proper notebook is a real pain. Let me share what has worked for me. I am a hands on tech with no real background in proper notetaking outside of some basic English classes 20+ years ago.

This info is not earth shattering, I am writing this to call you out on not having notes, at least good ones that is.

Pen and Paper or Digital?

This is a no brainer, you gotta go digital. There are many great applications for this that all have their own pros and cons. These are a few that I use:

If I had to recommend one to get started with and you are willing to be thrown into the deep end I would try out Clickup. I say deep end because it is not exactly for note taking. It is a super powerful organizational suite that offers something called ClickUp docs, which is awesome for notes. It syncs flawless between devices, has a desktop app and to put it simply it just works. There are several YouTube videos that explain the whole platform and are well worth checking out. You can insert code blocks and write in markdown, just I am doing here! Oh by the way ClickUp basic is free check it out and prepare to be amazed and a little intimidated ... https://clickup.com/features/docs

If you want something simpler that is also super reliable and focuses just on notes, Microsoft OneNote is a great option. Out of the box it is intuitive to use and their search function is probably the best out of these options. You can parse quicky between note books and sections with a super powerful global search.

Coda and Notion are similar on the surface. I am partial to Coda only because I have used it way more that notion and I like the Coda Packs that integrate easily with Google; notion has these things also, like I said just me choosing one...

So I have to type in everything manually?

Nope, not even close. Enter the screen snip tool. On windows it is [Start Key (on keyboard) + Shift + S], on Mac [Shift + Command + 5] that combination of keys is something you need to remember. Snip and paste in your favorite note taking application.

Pause a video, snip. See a great block of code on the Mozilla docs, snip. Type out something you need to remember in VS Code that was genius, snip. Snipping is where it all starts!

Now there is more to note taking than just snipping, and remember if it something you are going to publish don't forget to get the source info to give proper credit where credit is due! You can make a heading (that will eventually be on a table of contents), highlight code, drop it in a code block and some programs even allow syntax highlighting. This is from my javascript notebook in ClickUp.

Links oh links. Yep links are key, go ahead and embed or paste in the link to that favorite YouTube video that explains passed by value vs. passed by reference in JavaScript. Yea sure this is the last time you need to watch it because you got it this time, but just in case drop a link in your notes.

So that is it?

Yup! Copy paste text, screen snips, links and then just type out the rest. You will naturally develop the way you like to break things up. For example you could have a main page about JavaScript, then sub pages about array operations, DOM selectors, and event listeners. Divide these pages up with headings so you can jump to the area quickly, alternatively you can use the global search feature of many of these applications. Find what you need, hopefully it is in a nice code block that you copy and paste right into VScode!

In conclusion, if you find yourself googling the same thing more than once or researching new material try taking some notes.

Honestly it takes time to do this right but it is well worth it in the end.

Currently I am attending a coding bootcamp, I have two excellent instructors and a well written curriculum that are always linking to external references, these end up in my notes for when I need them at 3am....

Give it a try next time you are learning something new and be prepared to be proud of yourself!
item.author: jsignature

item.title: 负边距 | Negative margins
item.description:
最后，我们还有一个要考虑的因素：负边距。

负边距允许我们减少两个元素之间的空间。 它允许我们将一个孩子拉到其父母的边界框之外，或者减少兄弟姐妹之间的空间，直到它们重叠。

负利润率如何崩溃？ 嗯，它实际上与积极的非常相似！ 负边距将共享一个空间，该空间的大小由最重要的负边距决定。 在此示例中，元素重叠 75px，因为更负的边距 (-75px) 比另一个 (-25px) 更重要。

当负利润率和正利润率混合在一起时怎么办？ 在这种情况下，数字相加。 在这个例子中，-25px 负边距和 25px 正边距相互抵消，没有效果，因为 -25px + 25px 为 0。

为什么我们要应用没有效果的边距？！ 好吧，有时您无法控制两个边距之一。 也许它来自传统风格，或者它紧紧地嵌入在一个组件中。 通过对父级应用反向负边距，您可以“取消”边距。

当然，这并不理想。 最好删除不需要的边距，而不是添加更多边距！ 但是在某些情况下，这种 hacky 修复可以成为救命稻草。



原文:

Finally, we have one more factor to consider: negative margins.

Negative margins allow us to reduce the space between two elements. It lets us pull a child outside its parent's bounding box, or reduce the space between siblings until they overlap.

How do negative margins collapse? Well, it's actually quite similar to positive ones! The negative margins will share a space, and the size of that space is determined by the most significant negative margin. In this example, the elements overlap by 75px, since the more-negative margin (-75px) was more significant than the other (-25px).

What about when negative and positive margins are mixed? In this case, the numbers are added together. In this example, the -25px negative margin and the 25px positive margin cancel each other out and have no effect, since -25px + 25px is 0.

Why would we want to apply margins that have no effect?! Well, sometimes you don't control one of the two margins. Maybe it comes from a legacy style, or it's tightly ensconced in a component. By applying an inverse negative margin to the parent, you can "cancel out" a margin.

Of course, this is not ideal. Better to remove unwanted margins than to add even more margins! But this hacky fix can be a lifesaver in certain situations.
item.author: topcode007

item.title: 多个正负边距 | Multiple positive and negative margins
item.description:
我们已经深入了解了这里的杂草，我们还有一件事要看。 它是这个话题的“最终boss”，是我们迄今为止看到的所有规则的顶点。

如果我们有多个利润竞争同一个空间，有些是负数怎么办？

如果涉及的边距超过 2 个，则算法如下所示：

  • 找到最大的正边距
  • 找到最大的*负边距
  • 将这两个数字加在一起 ​​这是代码中的示例。

在这个例子中，我们最显着的正边距是 30px。 我们最显着的负边距是-20px。 因此，我们最终得到 10px 的已实现边距，因为我们将正负值加在一起。

(No 3D illustration for this one — honestly, it was too busy and chaotic-looking to offer much clarity 😅) （这个没有 3D 插图——老实说，它看起来太忙乱了，无法提供太多清晰度😅）

Gayatri Sachdeva - Gayatri Sachdeva - May 11 5月11日



原文:

We've gotten pretty deep into the weeds here, and we have one more thing to look at. It's the "final boss" of this topic, the culmination of all the rules we've seen so far.

What if we have multiple margins competing for the same space, and some are negative?

If there are more than 2 margins involved, the algorithm looks like this:

  • Find the largest positive margin
  • Find the largest* negative margin
  • Add those two numbers together Here's an example in code.

In this example, our most significant positive margin is 30px. Our most significant negative margin is -20px. Therefore, we wind up with 10px of realized margin, since we add the positive and negative values together.

(No 3D illustration for this one — honestly, it was too busy and chaotic-looking to offer much clarity 😅)

Gayatri Sachdeva - May 11
item.author: topcode007

item.title: 您为自己构建的最喜欢的软件是什么？ | What's your favorite piece of software that you built for yourself?
item.description: 原文:
item.author: waylonwalker

item.title: #20：使用 SWA CLI 部署 | #20: Deploy with SWA CLI
item.description:
欢迎来到#30DaysOfSWA的Week 3, Day 6天！

这是 2 部分系列的第二部分，重点介绍在 2022 年 5 月 19 日的Azure 静态 Web 应用程序周年纪念活动上发布Azure 静态 Web 应用程序 CLI 。昨天的帖子介绍了核心swa命令并介绍了swa init和swa start命令 - 和涵盖的配置。

今天，我们将探索swa login和swa deploy命令，它们有助于将本地开发的应用程序部署到 Azure 云。 我们将介绍使用环境变量来进一步简化开发人员体验。

我们将涵盖的内容

  • 登录到 Azure
  • 部署您的应用
  • 环境变量
  • 练习：使用新的swa CLI 进行身份验证并部署您的应用程序

关于作者

在@manekinekko 找到他或在dev.to上关注他。

在@sinedied 找到他或在dev.to上关注他。

对于关于 SWA CLI 的第二篇文章，我们将讨论新的部署选项。 是的，您现在可以直接从命令行部署您的应用程序！ 谁能比实施它的人更好地谈论它？ 我会让我的朋友瓦西姆向你解释一切。

如果您从未听说过静态 Web 应用程序（或简称 SWA），它是 Azure 提供的 Web 应用程序托管服务，提供简化的全栈开发，具有许多内置功能，如身份验证、可自定义路由、无服务器功能等。 它还有一个很棒的免费套餐😉

登录到 Azure

在用于 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序的新 SWA CLI 中，我们引入了一个新的swa login命令，允许您直接从命令行登录到您的 Azure 帐户。 此命令将加密您的凭据并将其存储在系统的密钥链中，因此您不必再次输入它们。

在最常见的情况下， swa login将从以下位置（按此顺序）选择您当前的活动 Azure 凭据：

  1. 您的环境变量，如果您设置了以下一个或所有变量：
    • AZURE_TENANT_ID
    • AZURE_CLIENT_ID
    • AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET
  2. 你当前的 Visual Studio Code，如果你使用的是 VS Code 并且连接到 Azure。
  3. 使用浏览器的交互式提示。
  4. 最后一个回退是设备代码流，它将提示您使用生成的设备代码登录到您的 Azure 帐户。

但是，如果需要，您还可以使用可用标志从命令行提供任何这些凭据：

 Usage: swa login [ options] login into Azure Options: -S , --subscription-id <subscriptionId> Azure subscription ID used by this project ( default: "process.env.AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID" ) -R , --resource-group <resourceGroupName> Azure resource group used by this project -T , --tenant-id <tenantId> Azure tenant ID ( default: "process.env.AZURE_TENANT_ID" ) -C , --client-id <clientId> Azure client ID -CS , --client-secret <clientSecret> Azure client secret -n , --app-name <appName> Azure Static Web App application name -cc , --clear-credentials clear persisted credentials before login ( default: false ) -u , --use-keychain enable using the operating system native keychain for persistent credentials ( default: true ) -nu , --no-use-keychain disable using the operating system native keychain

登录后，SWA CLI 会将您的活动 Azure 订阅 ID 和租户 ID 存储在项目根目录下名为.env的本地文件中：

 ➜ swa login Welcome to Azure Static Web Apps CLI ( 1.0.0 ) Checking Azure session... ✔ Successfully logged into Azure! ✔ Saved project credentials in .env file. ✔ Successfully setup project!

使用swa deploy命令将项目部署到 Azure 时将使用此文件。

部署您的应用

新的 SWA CLI 使将应用程序部署到 Azure 变得更加容易。 您现在可以使用一个swa deploy命令直接从命令行部署您的应用程序。

否则，它将即时创建一个新的 - 或提示您从已经可用的列表中选择一个：

 ➜ swa deploy Welcome to Azure Static Web Apps CLI ( 1.0.0 ) Using configuration "thundr" from file: /home/wassimchegham/oss/@thundr-dev/thundr/swa-cli.config.json Deploying front-end files from folder: /home/wassimchegham/oss/@thundr-dev/thundr/app/dist/thundr-ui Deploying API from folder: /home/wassimchegham/oss/@thundr-dev/thundr/api Checking Azure session... ✔ Successfully logged into Azure! Checking project settings... ? Choose your Static Web App › - Use arrow-keys. Return to submit. ❯ >> Create a new application swa_samples/angular-web-bluetooth swa_samples/catsify swa_samples/hexa swa_samples/ngxtools swa_samples/nitrooo ↓ swa_samples/photobooth-teams

从 CI/CD 管道部署

从 CI/CD 管道进行部署是许多项目中的常见做法。 可以使用swa deploy命令将应用从 CI/CD 管道部署到 Azure。 您可以通过设置部署令牌来完成此操作：

 ➜ swa deploy --print-token --app-name thundr --resource-group swa_samples Welcome to Azure Static Web Apps CLI ( 1.0.0 ) Checking project "thundr" settings... ✔ Successfully setup project! Deploying to environment: preview Deployment token: 7c3fc44b858164b677-truncated-8c80ace9-39a8-416c-ae22-864745c0470b003
 swa deploy --deployment-token = 7c3fc44b858164b677-truncated-8c80ace9-39a8-416c-ae22-864745c0470b003b003

您还可以将部署令牌设置为环境变量：

 export SWA_CLI_DEPLOYMENT_TOKEN = 7c3fc44b858164b677-truncated-8c80ace9-39a8-416c-ae22-864745c0470b003b003 swa deploy

环境变量

新的 SWA CLI 引入了一组新的环境变量，使您可以更轻松地配置您的体验。 您可以通过设置以下环境变量来自定义 SWA CLI 的行为：

  • 通用设置
  • 模拟器设置
  • 部署设置
  • 运行时设置
  • 登录体验

您可以在我们新文档网站的环境变量部分阅读更多关于这些环境变量的信息。

下一步

从您的项目开始到部署到 Azure，中间有所有需要的本地测试，您现在应该能够从命令行处理完整的开发过程。 现在剩下的主要挑战是部署和发展您的应用程序😉。

如果您想了解一些可以在开发应用程序时使用的技巧，可以查看静态 Web 应用程序 - 技巧和窍门视频系列。

您还可以通过查看新的文档网站了解有关 SWA CLI 的所有新功能以及如何使用它们的更多信息。

告诉我们您的反馈！

我们也在 dev.to 上尽我们所能倾听和帮助！ 请告诉我们您的经验、您的困难、您的问题以及您希望在 SWA CLI 的下一版本中看到哪些功能。

资源

想要查看静态 Web 应用程序 CLI 的运行情况吗？ 该工具于昨天正式发布，在Azure 静态 Web 应用程序周年纪念日上发布。 观看该片段以了解 SWA CLI 的运行情况。



原文:

Welcome to Week 3, Day 6 of #30DaysOfSWA!!

This is second part of the 2-part series that focuses on the launch of the Azure Static Web Apps CLI at the Azure Static Web Apps Anniversary event on May 19, 2022. Yesterday's post introduced the core swa command and walked through the swa init and swa start commands - and covered configuration.

Today, we'll explore the swa login and swa deploy commands that help with deploying your locally-developed app to the Azure cloud. And we'll cover the use of Environment Variables to streamline the developer experience further.

What We'll Cover

  • Logging in to Azure
  • Deploying your app
  • Environment variables
  • Exercise: Use the new swa CLI to authenticate, and deploy your app

About the Author(s)

Wassim Chegham is a Senior Cloud Advocate on the Developer Relations team at Microsoft and one of the Core Contributors to the Azure Static Web Apps CLI open source project. Find him at @manekinekko or follow him right here on dev.to.

Yohan Lasorsa is a Senior Cloud Advocate on the Developer Relations team at Microsoft and one of the Core Contributors to the Azure Static Web Apps CLI open source project. Find him at @sinedied or follow him right here on dev.to.

For this second article about the SWA CLI, we'll talk about the new deployment options. And yes, you can now deploy your apps straight from the command line! Who's better than the person who implemented it to talk about it? I'll let my friend Wassim explain it all to you.

If you've never heard of Static Web Apps (or SWA for short), it's a web app hosting service provided by Azure, offering streamlined full-stack development with many built-in features like authentication, customizable routing, serverless functions and more. It also has a great free tier 😉

Logging in to Azure

In the new SWA CLI for Azure Static Web Apps, we introduced a new swa login command allowing you to log in to your Azure account, straight from the command line. This command will encrypt and store your credentials in your system's key Chain, so you don't have to enter them again.

In most common cases, the swa login will pick your current active Azure credentials from the following locations (in this order):

  1. Your environment variables, if you have set one or all of following variables:
    • AZURE_TENANT_ID
    • AZURE_CLIENT_ID
    • AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET
  2. Your current Visual Studio Code, if you're using VS Code and is connected to Azure.
  3. Interactive prompt using your browser.
  4. The last fallback is the device code flow, which will prompt you to log in to your Azure account using a generated device code.

However, if you need, you can also provide any of these credentials from the command line, using the available flags:

Usage: swa login [options] login into Azure Options: -S, --subscription-id <subscriptionId> Azure subscription ID used by this project (default: "process.env.AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID") -R, --resource-group <resourceGroupName> Azure resource group used by this project -T, --tenant-id <tenantId> Azure tenant ID (default: "process.env.AZURE_TENANT_ID") -C, --client-id <clientId> Azure client ID -CS, --client-secret <clientSecret> Azure client secret -n, --app-name <appName> Azure Static Web App application name -cc, --clear-credentials clear persisted credentials before login (default: false) -u, --use-keychain enable using the operating system native keychain for persistent credentials (default: true) -nu, --no-use-keychain                    disable using the operating system native keychain

Once logged in, the SWA CLI will store your active Azure subscription ID and tenant ID in a local file called .env at the root of your project:

➜ swa login Welcome to Azure Static Web Apps CLI (1.0.0) Checking Azure session...
✔ Successfully logged into Azure!
✔ Saved project credentials in .env file.
✔ Successfully setup project!

This file will be used when deploying your project to Azure using the swa deploy command.

Deploying your app

The new SWA CLI makes it even more easier to deploy your apps to Azure. You can now deploy your app directly from the command line, using one single swa deploy command.

The swa deploy command is smarter enough to figure out if you already have an Azure Static Web Apps instance available, and if so, it will use it. Otherwise, it will create a new one on the fly - or prompt you to select one from a list of already available ones:

➜ swa deploy Welcome to Azure Static Web Apps CLI (1.0.0) Using configuration "thundr" from file: /home/wassimchegham/oss/@thundr-dev/thundr/swa-cli.config.json Deploying front-end files from folder: /home/wassimchegham/oss/@thundr-dev/thundr/app/dist/thundr-ui Deploying API from folder: /home/wassimchegham/oss/@thundr-dev/thundr/api Checking Azure session...
✔ Successfully logged into Azure! Checking project settings...
? Choose your Static Web App › - Use arrow-keys. Return to submit.
❯ >> Create a new application
    swa_samples/angular-web-bluetooth
    swa_samples/catsify
    swa_samples/hexa
    swa_samples/ngxtools
    swa_samples/nitrooo
  ↓ swa_samples/photobooth-teams

The swa deploy command is also able to detect if your existing Static Web Apps instance has already been deployed from a CI/CD pipeline and inform you about it!

Deploying from a CI/CD pipeline

Deploying from CI/CD pipelines is a common practice in many projects. You can use the swa deploy command to deploy your app to Azure from your CI/CD pipeline. You can accomplish this by setting a deployment token:

➜ swa deploy --print-token --app-name thundr --resource-group swa_samples Welcome to Azure Static Web Apps CLI (1.0.0) Checking project "thundr" settings...
✔ Successfully setup project!

Deploying to environment: preview

Deployment token:
7c3fc44b858164b677-truncated-8c80ace9-39a8-416c-ae22-864745c0470b003

swa deploy --deployment-token=7c3fc44b858164b677-truncated-8c80ace9-39a8-416c-ae22-864745c0470b003b003

You can also set the deployment token as an environment variable:

export SWA_CLI_DEPLOYMENT_TOKEN=7c3fc44b858164b677-truncated-8c80ace9-39a8-416c-ae22-864745c0470b003b003
swa deploy

Environment variables

The new SWA CLI introduced a new set of environment variables to make it easier for you to configure your experience. You can customize the behavior of the SWA CLI by setting the following environment variables:

  • General settings
  • Emulator settings
  • Deploy settings
  • Runtime settings
  • Login experience

You can read more about these environment variables in the Environment Variables section in our new documentation website.

Next steps

From the start of your project to its deployment to Azure, with all the needed local testing in-between, you should now be equipped to take care of your complete development process all from the command line. Now the main challenge remaining for you is to deploy and grow your app 😉.

If you'd like to learn some tips you can use when developing your app, you can check out the Static Web Apps - Tips & Tricks video series.

You can also learn more about all the new features of the SWA CLI and how to use them by looking at the new docs website.

Tell us your feedback!

We're also here on dev.to to listen and help as much as we can! 🙂

Please tell us your experience, your difficulties, your questions, and what features you would like to see in the next versions of the SWA CLI.

Resources

Want to see the Static Web Apps CLI in action? The tool became generally available yesterday, launching at the Azure Static Web Apps Anniversary. Watch the segment to see the SWA CLI in action.
item.author: nitya

item.title: 🧵 评价我的 GitHub 个人资料 | 🧵 Rate my GitHub Profile
item.description:
维丹查纳尼

发表于所以今天我才看到我的 GitHub Profile 时隔很久，看起来不太好。 我已经很久没有更新我的个人资料了，所以我决定即兴创作我的 GitHub 个人资料。

我是电脑游戏的超级粉丝，而RGB是游戏玩家的初恋，所以我将主题保留为 RGB。 我更新了我的目标，制作了一些新的工作流程，添加了新的 gif 和新的动画徽章，让它看起来更生动。

我很想对我的个人资料有一些反馈，它可以是任何类型的，如建议或更改，甚至是差评。
如果您花时间并对此发表评论，那将意义重大。

🩰 The Flying Shooter ❤️‍🔥 - 🩰飞行射手❤️‍🔥 - Apr 26 4月26日



原文:

Vedant Chainani

Posted on May 21

🖥️ Computer Geek , ☕ Coffee Addict , Currently Learning 🔗 Blockchain and Solidity - Envoy-VC

So today I just saw my GitHub Profile after a Long time and it didn't look that good. It had been a long time since I Updated My Profile so,I decided to Improvise my GitHub Profile.

I am a Huge fan of Computer Games and RGB is the first love of a Gamer so I Kept the theme as RGB. I updated my Goals, made some new workflows, added new gifs and new animated Badges to make it look more lively.

I would love to have some feedback on my Profile, It can be of any type like suggestions or changes or even bad reviews.
It would mean a Lot if you gave your time and commented anything about it.

🩰 The Flying Shooter ❤️‍🔥 - Apr 26
item.author: envoy_

item.title: 2022 年适合初学者的 6 个最佳 TypeScript 教程 | 6 Best TypeScript Tutorials For Beginners in 2022
item.description:
简而言之，TypeScript 是具有可选类型的 JS 的超集，它可以编译成常规的 JavaScript。 与 CoffeeScript（添加语法糖）或 PureScript（根本不像 JavaScript）等其他语言不同，您无需学习太多即可开始编写 TypeScript 代码。

为什么要学习 Typescript？

无论你是专业的前端开发人员还是立志成为一名前端开发人员，TypeScript 都非常值得学习。 它可以为你开辟更多的工作机会。 此外，它将使您对 JavaScript 有更好的理解和新的视角。

最佳初学者打字稿课程列表

披露：这篇文章包括附属链接； 如果您通过本文中的链接购买产品/服务，我们可能会收到补偿。

最佳 TypeScript 在线课程认证列表

学习使用来自 Microsoft 的强大类型语言来增强您的 Web 应用程序。

本课程将向您介绍 TypeScript，这是一种受微软 JavaScript 启发的语言。 学生将在 TypeScript 入门课程中学习 TypeScript 的基础知识，以提高初级工作技能。

在本 Typescript 课程中，您将：

  • 在您的工作站上编译、测试和运行 TypeScript。
  • 使用 TypeScript 类和类型注释。
  • 使用 TypeScript 构建并运行一个简单的应用程序。

作为第一步，你会发现 TypeScript 最适合什么。 此外，我们还将讨论谁制作了 TypeScript，以及每天有哪些项目依赖它。

第二部分解释了如何使用 Node.js 和 NPM 安装 TypeScript 并使用命令行将 TypeScript 编译成 JavaScript。 此外，您还将深入了解 TypeScript 编译器错误。

最后，您将了解 TypeScript 的众多功能中的一些。

课程评分： 4.2 分，满分 5.0 分（共 8,031 分）
时长： 1 小时
证书：购买证书

本 Typescript 课程的主题包括：

  • 类型
  • 接口
  • 使用类
  • 命名空间
  • 模块导出

本 Typescript 课程将为您介绍该语言的基础知识，向您展示 TypeScript 是什么以及如何利用它来发挥您的优势。

课程评分： 4.4 分，满分 5.0 分（总共 1,100 分）
时长： 3 小时 35 分钟
证书：购买证书

了解基础知识、其功能、工作流程以及如何使用它。

本课程涵盖了 TypeScript 的基础知识及其最重要的特性（类型！），以便您可以将其应用到您的任何项目中。

在本 TypeScript 课程中，您将：

  • 在他们的任何项目中使用 TypeScript 及其特性，如类型、ES6 支持、类、模块、接口等等。
  • 了解 TypeScript 的真正含义及其工作原理。
  • 了解为什么 TypeScript 比普通 JavaScript 具有真正的优势。
  • 学习 TypeScript 在理论上以及应用于实际用例和项目。
  • 了解如何将 TypeScript 与 ReactJS 或 NodeJS / Express 结合使用。

这不仅仅是基础知识！ 此外，您将了解高级功能（如装饰器）以及如何使用 TypeScript 设置工作流程。 本课程涵盖了 TypeScript 工作流程和 webpack 工作流程。

课程评分： 4.7 分，满分 5.0 分（总共 31,256 分）
时长： 15 小时
证书：结业证书

了解如何在 JavaScript 应用程序中利用 TypeScript 语言的全部功能。

你可以像使用 TypeScript 一样编写 JavaScript。 因为 TypeScript 编译为普通的 JavaScript，所以它可以在任何浏览器、主机或操作系统上运行。

本 Typescript 课程的主题包括：

  • 将 TypeScript 引入您的应用程序

  • 基本的 TypeScript 用法

  • 定义更复杂的类型

  • 从现有类型扩展和提取元数据

  • 使用装饰器添加动态行为

  • 使用模块

在本课程中，您将学习如何在 JavaScript 应用程序中利用 TypeScript 语言的全部功能。 此外，在继续讨论特定于 TypeScript 的类型、类、泛型、模块和装饰器之前，您将重新审视一些 JavaScript 基础知识。

课程评分： 4.5 分（满分 5.0 分）（总注册人数 2,848）
时长： 2 小时 15 分钟
证书：结业证书

了解 TypeScript 最重要的功能，并快速提高您使用该语言的效率。

在本课程中，您将了解 TypeScript 的所有最重要功能，这将使您快速高效地工作。

本 TypeScript 课程的主题包括：

  • 安装 TypeScript 和配置项目
  • 利用内置类型
  • 使用 TypeScript 编写更好的函数
  • 创建和使用自定义类型
  • 创建和使用模块
  • 使用类型声明文件提高工作效率

您将从学习如何设置 TypeScript 项目开始。 之后，您将学习如何设计和使用您的类型。 最后，您将学习如何为您能想象到的几乎所有 JavaScript 库添加强大的类型支持。

完成本课程后，您将基本掌握 TypeScript 最重要的功能，以及开始使用 TypeScript 开发客户端 Web 应用程序和 NodeJS 应用程序所需的技能。

课程评分： 4.3 分，满分 5.0 分（共 357 分）
时长： 3 小时
证书：结业证书

通过将 TypeScript 集成到您现有的 JavaScript 应用程序中，编写更易于维护、可重用的代码。

在本课程中，您将学习如何设置 TypeScript 并使用它来简单地改进现有的 JavaScript 项目。 除了定义自定义类型、将现有 JavaScript 类转换为 TypeScript 类以及使用访问修饰符封装逻辑和数据之外，您还将学习如何定义自定义类型结构。

本 TypeScript 课程的主题包括：

  • 基础

  • 定义自定义类型

  • 课程

  • 扩展和改进类型支持

此外，您将学习如何利用泛型重用代码、类型检查文件以及向 API 添加类型支持。 因此，通过使用这些方法，您可以成为更有生产力和效率的 JavaScript 开发人员。

课程评分： 4.7 / 5.0（17,410 总注册人数）
时长： 1 小时 8 分钟
证书：结业证书

嘿！ 我们希望您发现这些在线打字稿课程和认证列表对您有帮助且有趣。 既然你已经做到了这一步，那么你肯定愿意学习更多，在 Coursesity，我们有责任用他们愿意学习的主题的知识来启发人们。



原文:

In short, TypeScript is a superset of JS with optional typing, and it compiles down to regular JavaScript. Unlike other languages such as CoffeeScript (which adds syntactic sugar) or PureScript (which does not resemble JavaScript at all), you do not have to learn much to start writing TypeScript code.

Why should you learn Typescript?

Whether you're a professional front-end developer or aspiring to become one, TypeScript is well worth learning. It can open up more job opportunities for you. Moreover, it will give you a better understanding and a new perspective on JavaScript.

Best Typescript Courses For Beginners List

Disclosure: This post includes affiliate links; we may receive compensation if you purchase products/services from the links in this article.

Best TypeScript Online Courses Certifications List

1. Introduction to TypeScript - (Free)

Learn to supercharge your web-applications with the powerful typed language from Microsoft.

This course will introduce you to TypeScript, Microsoft's JavaScript-inspired language. Students will learn the basics of TypeScript for junior workplace skills during the introductory TypeScript course.

In this Typescript course, you will:

  • Compile, Test and Run TypeScript on your workstation.
  • Use TypeScript classes and type annotations.
  • Build and run a simple app using TypeScript.

As a first step, you will discover what TypeScript is best suited for. In addition, we will discuss who makes TypeScript and what projects rely on it every day.

item.title: 冒泡排序算法 | Bubble Sort Algorithm
item.description:
索海卜达尔

发表于因此，如果列表 A 中有 n 个元素，则将 A[0] 与 A[1] 进行比较，将 A[1] 与 A[2] 进行比较，依此类推。 因此，在每次迭代结束时，列表中最大的元素会冒泡。

因此冒泡排序中的比较总数为： Sum = (N-1) + (N-2) + (N-3)+ ... + 3 + 2 + 1 = N(N-1)/2

因此，冒泡排序技术的各种复杂性如下所示：

最坏情况时间复杂度O(n^2)


最佳情况时间复杂度O(n)
平均时间复杂度O(n^2)
空间复杂度O(1)

现在，计算排序数组所需的比较总数 = (n-1) + (n-2) + (n-3) + 。 对于最坏情况： •交换总数 = 比较总数 •比较总数（最坏情况）= n(n-1)/2

最坏情况和平均情况时间复杂度：O(n^2)。最坏情况发生在数组反向排序时。

对于最佳情况： • 冒泡排序技术只需要一个额外的内存空间用于临时变量以促进交换。 请注意，冒泡排序技术的最佳情况时间复杂度将是列表已经排序时，这将是 O (n)。

让我们考虑一个值为 {11, 17, 18, 26, 23} 的数组
下面，我们有一个图形表示优化的冒泡排序将如何对给定的数组进行排序。

如我们所见，在第一次迭代中，发生了交换，因此我们将标志值更新为 1，结果，执行再次进入 for 循环。
但是在第二次迭代中，不会发生交换，因此flag的值将保持为0，执行将跳出循环。

时间和空间复杂度：时间复杂度：O(n^2)。 空间复杂度：O(n)。 排序是否在冒泡排序中进行？
因此冒泡排序算法是一种就地算法。

冒泡排序算法稳定吗？
冒泡排序算法在哪里使用？
源代码在我的 Github
任何人都可以编辑或优化代码，我希望修订有所帮助并变得更好。

谢谢你。
 索海布

玛雅克·阿罗拉—— 玛雅克·阿罗拉—— 玛雅克·阿罗拉—— 拉尔斯·瓦希特 - Dec 23 '21 21 年 12 月 23 日



原文:

Sohaib Dar

Posted on May 21

1. Bubble Sort Technique •Bubble Sort is the simplest of the sorting techniques. •In the bubble sort technique, each of the elements in the list is compared to its adjacent element. Thus if there are n elements in list A, then A[0] is compared to A[1], A[1] is compared to A[2] and so on. •After comparing if the first element is greater than the second, the two elements are swapped then.

•Using the bubble sort technique, sorting is done in passes or iteration. Thus at the end of each iteration, the largest element in the list bubbles up.

2. Complexity Analysis Of The Bubble Sort Algorithm
•In bubble sort, we make N-1 comparisons in the first pass, N-2 comparisons in the second pass and so on.

Hence the total number of comparisons in bubble sort is: Sum = (N-1) + (N-2) + (N-3)+ … + 3 + 2 + 1 = N(N-1)/2

= O(n^2) => Time complexity of bubble sort technique

Thus the various complexities for bubble sort technique are given below:

Worst case time complexity O(n^2)


Best case time complexity O(n)
Average time complexity O(n^2)
Space complexity O(1)

Now , calculating total number of comparison required to sort the array = (n-1) + (n-2) + (n-3) + . . . 2 + 1

= (n-1)(n-1+1)/2 {by using sum of N natural Number formula*}

=n (n-1)/2

For Worst case: •Total number of swaps = Total number of comparison •Total number of comparison (Worst case) = n(n-1)/2

•Total number of swaps (Worst case) = n(n-1)/2

Worst and Average Case Time Complexity: O(n^2).The worst case occurs when an array is reverse sorted.

For Best Case: •The bubble sort technique requires only a single additional memory space for the temp variable to facilitate swapping. •The space complexity for bubble sort algorithm is O (1).

Note that the best case time complexity for bubble sort technique will be when the list is already sorted and that will be O (n).

3. **Optimized Bubble Sort** •To optimize our bubble sort algorithm, we can introduce a flag to monitor whether elements are getting swapped inside the inner for loop. •In the inner for loop, we check whether swapping of elements is taking place or not, everytime.

•If for a particular iteration, no swapping took place, it means the array has been sorted and we can jump out of the for loop, instead of executing all the iterations.

Let's consider an array with values {11, 17, 18, 26, 23}
Below, we have a pictorial representation of how the optimized bubble sort will sort the given array.

As we can see, in the first iteration, swapping took place, hence we updated our flag value to 1, as a result, the execution enters the for loop again.
But in the second iteration, no swapping will occur, hence the value of flag will remain 0, and execution will break out of loop.

*4. Recursive Bubble sort *

•We will mimic the iterative approach of the bubble sort to implement it recursively. •We will create a function for sorting in which we will check if the array length is 1 then return the array. •Else loop till the given length and swap the elements depending on their order.

•Recursively call the same function again with one element less.

Time and Space complexity: Time complexity: O(n^2). Space complexity: O(n). •We are calling the same function recursively for each element of the array and inside the function, we are looping till the given length of the array, So Time complexity is O(n ^ n) = O(n ^ 2).

•We are recursively calling the function for each element of the array, So space complexity is O(n).

5. Conclusion

•The main advantage of Bubble Sort is the simplicity of the algorithm. •In bubble sort, with every pass / iterator, the largest element bubbles up to the end of the list if the array is sorted in ascending order. •For the list to be sorted in descending order, the smallest element will be in its proper place at the end of every pass. •Bubble sort is usually taken for introducing sorting to the audience. •Bubble sort is also used in application like Computer graphics wherein filling of polygon edges.

Require bubble sort to sort the vertices lining the polygon.

Does sorting happens in place in Bubble sort?
>> Yes, Bubble sort performs swapping of adjacent pairs without use of any major data structure. Hence Bubble sort algorithm is an in-place algorithm.

Is Bubble sort algorithm stable?
>> Yes, bubble sort algorithm is stable.

Where is Bubble sort algorithm used?
>> Due to its simplicity, bubble sort is often used to introduce the concept of a sorting algorithm.

In computer graphics, it is popular for its capability to detect a very small error (like a swap of just two elements) in almost-sorted arrays and fix it with just linear complexity (2n).

The source Code is on My Github
•Bubble Sort: [((https://github.com/SohaibDar61/Algorithms/tree/main/Sorting%20Algorithms/Bubble%20Sort)]

•More Articles : [(https://www.linkedin.com/in/sohaibdar61)
•E-mail: eng.sohaibdar@gmail.com
•Phone:+201018741441
•Upwork: [(https://www.upwork.com/freelancers/~019e71f2adc499c4e8)]
•LinkedIn: [(https://www.linkedin.com/in/sohaibdar61)]

Anyone can edit or optimize the code , and I hope that revision helps and gets better.

Thank You.
Sohaib

Mayank Arora - Dec 24 '21

Mayank Arora - Dec 24 '21

Mayank Arora - Dec 24 '21

Lars Wächter - Dec 23 '21

item.title: 🔐 Laravel 文件权限设置 | 🔐 Laravel File Permissions Setup
item.description:
卡洛斯桑托斯

发表于你好，我想提供一个简单直接的方式来设置你的 laravel 项目权限。 这个例子就像一个适用于所有情况的工作。 您将不得不考虑您的服务器用户、配置等。

基本上有两种方法可以设置您的所有权和权限。 要么给自己所有权，要么让网络服务器成为所有文件的所有者。

假设 www-data （它可能是别的东西）是您的网络服务器用户。

 sudo chown -R www-data:www-data /path/to/your/laravel/root/directory

如果你这样做，网络服务器拥有所有文件，也是组，你将在上传文件或通过 FTP 处理文件时遇到一些问题，因为你的 FTP 客户端将以你的身份登录，而不是你的网络服务器，所以添加您的用户到 webserver 用户组：

 sudo usermod -a -G www-data ubuntu

当然，这假设您的网络服务器以 www-data（Homestead 默认）运行，并且您的用户是 ubuntu（如果您使用 Homestead，它就是 vagrant）。

然后将所有目录设置为 755，将文件设置为 644... SET 文件权限

sudo find /path/to/your/laravel/root/directory -type f -exec chmod 644 {} \;

设置目录权限

sudo find /path/to/your/laravel/root/directory -type d -exec chmod 755 {} \;

然后，您需要为网络服务器授予存储、缓存和网络服务器需要上传或写入的任何其他目录的读取和写入权限：

 sudo chgrp -R www-data storage bootstrap/cache sudo chmod -R ug+rwx storage bootstrap/cache

您的用户作为所有者（方便 STAGING 和 DEV）

如果你喜欢拥有所有的目录和文件（它使处理一切变得更容易），请转到你的 laravel 根目录：

 cd /var/www/html/laravel >> assuming this is your current root directory

然后你给你的用户和网络服务器权限：

 sudo find . -type f -exec chmod 664 {} \; sudo find . -type d -exec chmod 775 {} \;

结论

我希望你觉得这篇文章有帮助。

如果您有任何反馈或发现错误，请随时与我联系。

归功于：Andres Felipe 和 bgies 的 回答。

马塞洛·巴雷托·尼斯 - Apr 22 4月22日



原文:

Carlos Santos

Posted on May 21

Hi, I wanted to offer a brief and straight to the point way to setup your laravel project permissions. This examples are like a work for all case. You will have to taken into consideration your server users, configurations, etc. ‎‎

There are basically two ways to setup your ownership and permissions. Either you give yourself ownership or you make the webserver the owner of all files.

‏‏‎

Web Server as owner (Recommended for PROD)
Webserver as owner (the way most people do it, and the Laravel doc's way):

Assuming www-data (it could be something else) is your webserver user.

sudo chown -R www-data:www-data /path/to/your/laravel/root/directory

If you do that, the webserver owns all the files, and is also the group, and you will have some problems uploading files or working with files via FTP, because your FTP client will be logged in as you, not your webserver, so add your user to the webserver user group:

sudo usermod -a -G www-data ubuntu

Of course, this assumes your webserver is running as www-data (the Homestead default), and your user is ubuntu (it's vagrant if you are using Homestead).

Then you set all your directories to 755 and your files to 644... SET file permissions

sudo find /path/to/your/laravel/root/directory -type f -exec chmod 644 {} \;

Set directory permissions

sudo find /path/to/your/laravel/root/directory -type d -exec chmod 755 {} \;

Then you need to give read and write permissions to the webserver for storage, cache and any other directories the webserver needs to upload or write:

sudo chgrp -R www-data storage bootstrap/cache sudo chmod -R ug+rwx storage bootstrap/cache

‏‏‎

Your user as owner (Convenient for STAGING and DEV)

If you prefer to own all the directories and files (it makes working with everything much easier), go to your laravel root directory:

cd /var/www/html/laravel >> assuming this is your current root directory

‏‏‎ ‎ If you do that, the webserver owns all the files, and is also the group, and you will have some problems uploading files or working with files via FTP, because your FTP client will be logged in as you, not your webserver, so add your user to the webserver user group:

sudo usermod -a -G www-data ubuntu

sudo chown -R $USER:www-data .

Then you give your user and the webserver permissions:

sudo find . -type f -exec chmod 664 {} \; sudo find . -type d -exec chmod 775 {} \;

‏‏‎ Then you need to give read and write permissions to the webserver for storage, cache and any other directories the webserver needs to upload or write:

sudo chgrp -R www-data storage bootstrap/cache sudo chmod -R ug+rwx storage bootstrap/cache

‏

Conclusion

I hope you found this article helpful.

If you have any feedback or found mistakes, please don’t hesitate to reach out to me.

Credit to: Andres Felipe and bgies answers.

Marcelo Barreto Nees - Apr 22
item.author: sanzocarlos

item.title: 将缓存与 JavaScript 示例一起使用 | Using Cache with JavaScript examples
item.description:
在应用程序中，缓存策略性地将数据存储在内存中的某个位置，以减少延迟或避免花费不必要的资源。 它可以在离用户更近的服务器中，也可以在用户计算机中，以更快地访问特定数据。

使用缓存的权衡

与缓存一样，您将项目存储在内存中以便快速访问，它（自然）不具有数据持久性。 换句话说，如果您重置服务器，您将丢失数据。 通过将数据存储在内存中，由于存储容量比 HDD 低，它也更昂贵，因此对存储内容和存储时间有一个很好的策略。

缓存结构

在实现方面，您只需要知道缓存用作字典数据结构，使用键/值对存储项目。 例如，您可以通过使用其参数作为键来存储某个 http 请求的数据。

缓存示例用例

使用 3rd 方 API 为天气数据绘制天气应用程序图像，此 API 会根据请求向您收费。 为您的用户返回更新的数据是有意义的，但它是否足够关键到每秒准确？ 通过 TTL 为 60 秒的缓存，无论有多少用户在某个区域使用您的应用程序，您每分钟只需向 3rd 方 API 发出一个请求，同时让您的用户更新缓存数据。 这可以减少对该 API 的大量请求并为您节省资金，同时更快地将数据返回给用户。

简单的内存缓存示例

对于 Node.js 中的这个示例，我们将使用 node-cache 包来构建内存缓存。

 import axios from " axios " ; import NodeCache from " node-cache " ; class WeatherRepository { // Lets set the default TTL to 60 seconds, so the data will expire from the // cache in 60 seconds and we'll have to fetch the updated data from the API. weatherCache = new NodeCache ({ stdTTL : 60 }); async getCurrentWeatherInformation ( city ) { // First, lets check if the required data is in cache const cachedData = this . weatherCache . get ( city ); // If it is, return it if ( cachedData ) return cachedData ; const { data : weatherData } = axios . get ( `https://weather-api-url/?city= ${ city } ` ); // Else, fetch the data from the API then save it on the cache. // Here we'll use the city as key. this . weatherCache . set ( city , weatherData ); return weatherData ; } } export { WeatherRepository };

如果您需要一个功能比内存缓存更多的远程数据结构服务器，请寻找 Redis。

结论

缓存是一种非常用于减少延迟、存储最近的数据或可能被重用的数据的策略。 希望您发现这篇文章很有用，并将缓存添加到您的编程库中。 如果您有任何问题或建议，请告诉我！



原文:

In an application, caching is strategically storing data in memory somewhere to reduce latency or to avoid spending unnecessary resources. It can be in a server closer to the user or actually in the user computer to access specific data faster.

Trade-offs using Cache

As in cache you store items in memory for its fast access, it does not (naturally) have data persistency. In other words, if you reset the server you lose the data. Redis fix this issue by using memory dump to create a persistency snapshot.

By storing the data in memory, it is also more expensive due to the lower storage capacity than a HDD for example, so have a good strategy on what to store and for how long.

Cache Structure

Implementation wise, you only need to know that cache is used as a dictionary data structure, storing items with key/value pairs. For example, you can store data for certain http request by using its parameters as key.

Cache Example Use Case

Image a weather application using a 3rd party API for the weather data and this API charges you per request. Makes sense to return updated data for your user but is it critical enough to be accurate by each second? By having a cache with TTL of 60 seconds, no matter how many users are using your application in certain area, you would only make one request to the 3rd party API per minute while keeping your users updated with the cache data. This could reduce A LOT of requests to this API and save you money, while returning the data to the user way faster.

Simple in-memory cache example

For this example in Node.js we'll use the node-cache package to build an in-memory cache.

import axios from "axios";
import NodeCache from "node-cache"; class WeatherRepository { // Lets set the default TTL to 60 seconds, so the data will expire from the // cache in 60 seconds and we'll have to fetch the updated data from the API. weatherCache = new NodeCache({ stdTTL: 60 }); async getCurrentWeatherInformation(city) { // First, lets check if the required data is in cache const cachedData = this.weatherCache.get(city); // If it is, return it if (cachedData) return cachedData; const { data: weatherData } = axios.get( `https://weather-api-url/?city=${city}` ); // Else, fetch the data from the API then save it on the cache. // Here we'll use the city as key. this.weatherCache.set(city, weatherData); return weatherData; }
} export { WeatherRepository };

If you need a remote data structure server with way more features than an in-memory cache, look for Redis.

Conclusion

Cache is a strategy extremely used for reducing latency, storing recent data or data that is likely to be reused. Hope you find this article useful and add cache to your programming arsenal. Let me know if you have any questions or suggestions!
item.author: costamatheus97

item.title: Qu es un contexto en React | Que es un contexto en React
item.description:
问题

Tenemos una aplicación que renderiza una lista de usuarios y un buscador, el cual hace una búsqueda con base en el nombre de los usuarios. Tenemos una aplicación que renderiza una lista de usuarios y un buscador, el cual hace una búsqueda con base en el nombre de los usuarios。

Una forma de llevar a cabo esta aplicación es teniendo un estado para el valor del campo de búsqueda y utilizar este estado para hacer el filtrado en el array de usuarios. Una forma de llevar a cabo esta aplicación es teniendo un estado para el valor del campo de búsqueda y utilizar este estado para hacer el filtrado en el array de usuarios。

La estructura de la aplicación esta de la siguiente manera: La estructura de la aplicación esta de la siguiente manera:

 src/ ├── components/ │ ├── field/ │ │ ├── index.jsx │ │ └── field.module.css │ ├── searcher/ │ │ ├── index.jsx │ │ └── searcher.module.css │ └── user/ │ ├── index.jsx │ └── user.module.css ├── styles ├── app.jsx └── main.jsx

Y la composicion de componentes es la siguiente Y la compose de components es la siguiente

 // app.jsx < main > < Searcher /> </ main > // searcher.jsx < section > < Field /> </ section > // field.jsx < input />

Empecemos definiendo nuestro contexto, en este caso SearchContext . Empecemos definiendo nuestro contexto, en este caso SearchContext 。

 import { createContext } from 'react'; export const SearchContext = createContext();

Ahora debemos hallar la forma en que el componente Field pueda consumir el estado searchValue y la función onChangeSearch . Ahora debemos hallar la forma en que el componente Field pueda consumir el estado searchValue y la función onChangeSearch 。

Para ello, utilizaremos useContext en Field de la siguiente manera: Para ello, useContext en Field de la siguiente manera:

 import { useContext } from ' react ' ; import { SearchContext } from ' ~/contexts/search-context ' ; import styles from ' ./field.module.css ' ; const Field = () => { const { searchValue , onChangeSearch } = useContext ( SearchContext ); return ( < input type = "text" value = { searchValue } onChange = { onChangeSearch } placeholder = "Buscar por nombre" className = { styles . field } /> ); }; export default Field ;

Como vemos es importante pasarle como parámetro el contexto que queremos consumir a useContext . Como vemos es importante pasarle como parametro el contexto que queremos consumir a useContext 。

En este punto, aun no sabemos donde están definidos searchValue y onChangeSearch . En este punto，aun no sabemos donde están definidos searchValue y onChangeSearch 。

En este caso, lo propio es definir este objeto en app.jsx ya que es el componente donde se están definiendo el estado y el método que nos interesan. En este caso, lo propio es definir este objeto en app.jsx ya que es el componente donde se están definiendo el estado y el metodo que nos interesan。

Ahora para poder definir y compartir este objeto, debemos hacerlo a través de un proveedor, este proveedor se encuentra en nuestro objeto contexto que nos devolvió createContext y se utiliza de la siguiente manera: Ahora para poder definir y compartir este objeto, debemos hacerlo a través de unproveedor, esteproveedor se encuentra en nuestro objeto contexto que nos devolvió createContext y se utiliza de la siguiente manera:

 < SearchContext . Provider value = { { value : search , onChangeSearch } } > < Searcher /> </ SearchContext . Provider >

Como vemos nuestro proveedor tiene una propiedad llamada value la cual contiene el valor que el contexto compartirá. Como vemos nuestro proofedor tiene una propiedad llamada value la cual contiene el valor que el contexto compartirá。

El proveedor esta envolviendo al componente que queremos compartir dicha información. Elproveedor esta envolviendo al componente que queremos compartir dicha informationación。

De esta manera hemos logrado que Field altere el estado y se haga el filtrado de los usuarios. De esta manera hemos logrado que Field altere el estado y se haga el filtrado de los usuarios。

Les dejo final. Les dejo 决赛。

demo 演示



原文:

Cuando desarrollamos aplicaciones web basada en componentes, tenemos la necesidad de compartir información o funcionalidad a diferentes componentes en diferentes niveles de profundidad.

Si bien las propiedades en React nos ofrecen la posibilidad de resolver esta problema, esta solución pierde sentido (abierto a interpretaciones) entre mas niveles de profundidad de requiera recorrer.

Por ello React ofrece dos funciones para dar solución a este problema createContext y useContext.

Problema

Tenemos una aplicación que renderiza una lista de usuarios y un buscador, el cual hace una búsqueda con base en el nombre de los usuarios.

Una forma de llevar a cabo esta aplicación es teniendo un estado para el valor del campo de búsqueda y utilizar este estado para hacer el filtrado en el array de usuarios.

La estructura de la aplicación esta de la siguiente manera:

src/
├── components/
│   ├── field/
│   │   ├── index.jsx
│   │   └── field.module.css
│   ├── searcher/
│   │   ├── index.jsx
│   │   └── searcher.module.css
│   └── user/
│       ├── index.jsx
│       └── user.module.css
├── styles
├── app.jsx
└── main.jsx

Y la composicion de componentes es la siguiente

// app.jsx
<main> <Searcher />
</main> // searcher.jsx
<section> <Field />
</section> // field.jsx
<input />

Empecemos definiendo nuestro contexto, en este caso SearchContext.

import { createContext } from 'react';

export const SearchContext = createContext();

Ahora debemos hallar la forma en que el componente Field pueda consumir el estado searchValue y la función onChangeSearch.

Para ello, utilizaremos useContext en Field de la siguiente manera:

import { useContext } from 'react';
import { SearchContext } from '~/contexts/search-context';
import styles from './field.module.css'; const Field = () => { const { searchValue, onChangeSearch } = useContext(SearchContext); return ( <input type="text" value={searchValue} onChange={onChangeSearch} placeholder="Buscar por nombre" className={styles.field} /> );
}; export default Field;

Como vemos es importante pasarle como parámetro el contexto que queremos consumir a useContext.

En este punto, aun no sabemos donde están definidos searchValue y onChangeSearch.

En este caso, lo propio es definir este objeto en app.jsx ya que es el componente donde se están definiendo el estado y el método que nos interesan.

Ahora para poder definir y compartir este objeto, debemos hacerlo a través de un proveedor, este proveedor se encuentra en nuestro objeto contexto que nos devolvió createContext y se utiliza de la siguiente manera:

<SearchContext.Provider value={{ value: search, onChangeSearch }}> <Searcher />
</SearchContext.Provider>

Como vemos nuestro proveedor tiene una propiedad llamada value la cual contiene el valor que el contexto compartirá.

El proveedor esta envolviendo al componente que queremos compartir dicha información.

De esta manera hemos logrado que Field altere el estado y se haga el filtrado de los usuarios.

Les dejo final.

demo
item.author: davidponc

item.title: 如何减少编程面试的学习 | How to Make Studying for Programming Interviews Suck Less
item.description:
学习编程面试既痛苦又孤立。 当我经历这个过程时，我发现自己质疑是什么让我值得申请，即使我在这个行业取得进步的愿望已经占据了生活的方方面面。

我觉得好像我完全是一个人。 我不能和我的朋友谈论这件事，因为他们不明白我在学习什么，我也不能和我的同事交谈，因为我正试图离开公司。 就我一个人，我的头撞在电脑上试图解决 leetcode 问题。 最重要的是，有一千种不同的学习方法，每个人都试图告诉我做一些不同的事情。

燃尽

所有这些都导致了一种迷失的感觉，对我的学习方式缺乏信心。 然后我会进入倦怠期，放弃的想法是每天都会发生的事情。 对我来说，这个周期是 2 个月的生产力，然后是 1 个月的精疲力竭。 这是一种非常不健康的学习方式。 每次我感到这种倦怠时，我都会在一个月后回到正轨，感觉就像我失去了所有的进步。

建议

好消息是你不必经历我所经历的。 你可以用一种对你更健康的方式来做这件事。 记住这个建议在当下可能会感觉很困难，但它会推动你到达终点线。

攻击歧义

加入一个同样为编程面试而学习的人的社区，或者找一个学习伙伴。 对你所学的内容没有 100% 的信心？ 标记它。 与某人交谈。 不要让歧义蔓延。如果你经常在资源之间跳来跳去并且没有完成任何一个，这表明你有点迷失了。 制定计划并坚持下去。

善待自己

很容易开始让自己失望，并认为自己没有所需要的。 失败的 leetcode 并问自己“我怎么知道这个技巧？！” 每一天最终都会让你觉得自己不属于自己。 你确实属于。 要有耐心，并认识到你每天都露面是个坏蛋。 这狗屎很难，需要时间腌制。 您将 CS 学位塞进几个月。 不要太着急。 即使你的面试是在两周后，如果需要，你通常可以推迟为自己争取更多时间。

保持专注

有一百万件事可以吸引你的注意力。 构建随机项目、学习一门新语言、深入研究 Web3 等。花在其他事情上的时间并不是花在实现目标上的时间。 如果您对当前的方法感到沮丧，您可以随时混合您的学习方式（例如，如果 Leetcode 让您发疯，请切换到更多讨论和模拟）。

一致性

尤其是在动力下降期间，一致性是关键。 如果有一天你觉得自己很糟糕，那就做你的日常，然后注销。 不要太用力，因为它会追上你。 你的自然动力会回来，你只需要度过低谷，在此期间不要对自己太苛刻。

结论

这个过程对任何人来说都不容易。 如果是这样，每个人都将成为在谷歌工作的软件工程师。 请记住，困难只是意味着需要更多时间。

保持专注，攻击模棱两可，最重要的是善待自己。

加入社区

寻找那个学习伙伴或社区来追究你的责任？ 在这里申请： https ://www.hackpack.io/

大科技面试技巧通讯



原文:

Tldr: Stay focused, attack ambiguity, and most importantly be kind to yourself.

Studying for programming interviews is both painful and isolating. When I was going through the process, I found myself questioning what it is that makes me worthy to apply, even as my desire to progress in the industry was taking over every other aspect of life.

I felt as though I was completely alone. I couldn’t talk to my friends about it because they didn’t understand what I was learning and I couldn’t talk to my coworkers because I was trying to leave the company. It was just me, banging my head against the computer trying to solve leetcode problems. To top it all off, there were a thousand different study methods and everyone was trying to tell me to do something different.

Burnout

All of this led to a feeling of being lost with a lack of confidence in how I was studying. I would then enter periods of burnout, where the thought of giving up was an everyday occurrence. For me the cycle was 2 months of productivity and then 1 month of being burnt out. It was an incredibly unhealthy way to study. Each time I would feel this burnout I would get back on track a month later feeling like I had lost all of my progress.

Advice
